decision_summary: The chip temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the 85.0°C limit. Since material tuning has stalled, I will implement a coordinated geometry and material change: increase the heat spreader width to cover more lateral area and simultaneously increase its conductivity to maximize lateral heat flux away from the chip.

changes.reason: Widening the spreader to the domain limit (1.0m) increases the cross-sectional area for lateral heat spreading, reducing thermal resistance under the chip.
Increasing conductivity by ~25% works synergistically with the wider geometry to further lower the effective thermal resistance of the spreading layer.

expected_effects: Improve lateral heat distribution across the top boundary
risk_notes: Spreader width is now at the maximum domain limit; future geometry improvements must focus on height or position.
Conductivity increase is aggressive but within the 2x step limit; diminishing returns may occur if the base plate becomes the bottleneck.
